proposed_from.topic: Sharp non-equivalence/strict-extension theorem between three leading shift-share / formula IV estimators: the canonical Bartik-style shift-share IV (Borusyak, Hull, Jaravel 2022, Review of Economic Studies) used with shock-level identifying variation, the recentered-instrument variant (Borusyak and Hull 2023, Econometrica) that subtracts the expected exposure-weighted shock under randomized counterfactual shock realizations, and the formula-IV / design-based variant (Borusyak 2025, Econometrics Journal) that relaxes the shock-exogeneity moment to a covariate-conditional design-based moment. All three target the structural exposure coefficient beta_exp on the same DGP under shock independence + exposure additivity. The flagship question (axis b: non-equivalence frontier between named published shift-share estimators): characterize the sharp boundary in shock-correlation/dependency space at which the three estimators have asymptotically equivalent identifying moments vs. strictly different limit functionals, as a function of (i) the shock cross-correlation matrix Omega, (ii) the exposure-share covariance Pi, and (iii) the covariate-conditional shock-mean leakage rate epsilon. The kernel claim is a closed-form spectral non-equivalence theorem: the Bartik IF, the recentered-IV IF, and the Borusyak design-IF identify the same structural beta_exp if and only if a spectral condition lambda_max(Omega Pi) <= lambda_star holds, where lambda_star is a sharp threshold equal to the smallest singular value of an explicit observable kernel built from Pi and the design-leakage rate epsilon; when lambda_max(Omega Pi) > lambda_star, the Bartik estimator is biased proportional to the shock-correlation excess while the recentered-IV remains consistent and the design-IV achieves the semiparametric efficiency bound. lambda_star is a NEW mathematical object computable from observable shock cross-moments + exposure-share covariance + design-leakage rate, NOT an existence/absence statement from prior work. Recovers the classical Bartik consistency under uncorrelated shocks (lambda_max(Omega Pi)=0) and provides the first sharp triple-non-equivalence boundary for shift-share / formula IV in closed form.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue record is doc/API.md Section 3, which says the generated ExactID and PartialID catalogues are empty at seed time; no ThmSmith entry records shift-share, formula-IV, recentered-instrument, or design-conditioned formula-IV moments. The closest banked analogues are eid_lp_svar_nonequiv_v1, a non-equivalence proposal for LP versus non-Gaussian SVAR support sets, and eid_dml_orth_uniform_v1, a failed estimator-separation proposal whose reviewer trail requested finite-support matrix diagnostics. The gap is that neither contains the formula-IV objects q_B=0, q_R=m_R, q_D=m_D, the leakage vector epsilon=m_D-m_R, or the Pi-loaded null-space frontier for shift-share identification.","published_axis":"The closest published papers are BorusyakHullJaravel2022REStud, BorusyakHull2023Econometrica, and BorusyakHullJaravel2025ECTJ. BHJ 2022 establish the shock-level representation and sufficient consistency logic for canonical SSIV; BH 2023 define recentering by counterfactual shock means; BHJ 2025 review design-based formula-IV identification and extensions. The epsilon-gap is a single necessary-and-sufficient leakage geometry comparing the three named population moments under the same finite formula-IV DGP: recentered and design IV agree uniformly iff ||epsilon||_Pi=0, and canonical agrees as well iff ||m_D||_Pi=0."}
seed_list: Prior work BorusyakHullJaravel2022REStud/BorusyakHull2023Econometrica/BorusyakHullJaravel2025ECTJ establishes estimator-specific validity conditions; our seed conjectures that the three moments are equivalent iff a spectral Omega-Pi-epsilon threshold is not crossed, because correlated shocks can be absorbed by the design kernel only below a computable singular-value boundary.

Prior work AdaoKolesarMorales2019QJE would predict share-induced correlation is primarily an inference problem; our seed conjectures that some correlated-shock directions are harmless for identification while others move the SSIV estimand, because only directions outside the residual exposure kernel survive aggregation through Pi.

Prior work BorusyakHull2023Econometrica assumes recentering removes nonrandom exposure; our seed conjectures that recentering absorbs exactly the leakage directions in a design-null space and fails on the orthogonal Pi-loaded leakage directions, because counterfactual-shock means need not span all covariate-conditional leakage.

Prior work BorusyakHullJaravel2022REStud treats controls through structured shock-level residualization; our seed conjectures that high-dimensional controls can move an estimator across the equivalence frontier, because partialling out changes Pi and epsilon jointly rather than only precision.

Prior work BorusyakHullJaravel2025ECTJ describes optimal formula-IV construction; our seed conjectures a three-way efficiency frontier among canonical, recentered, and design IV under correlated shocks, because covariance weighting changes both the tangent space and the first-stage exposure strength.

Prior work GoldsmithPinkhamSorkinSwift2020AER would interpret Bartik validity through exposure-share exogeneity and Rotemberg weights; our seed conjectures that the share-exogeneity and shock-exogeneity routes diverge exactly when the leading Omega-Pi eigenvector has nonzero Rotemberg leverage, because the same shares can be valid weights but invalid shock aggregators.

Prior work JaegerRuistStuhler2018NBER warns that stable immigrant shares conflate short- and long-run responses; our seed conjectures that this dynamic conflation is the leading-eigenvector witness of the shift-share non-equivalence frontier, because serially persistent origin shocks load on the same Pi direction across periods.

Prior work BorusyakHullJaravel2025ECTJ notes multiple formula instruments and overidentification; our seed conjectures that overidentifying restrictions collapse or become informative according to the rank of Pi^{1/2} Omega Pi^{1/2} relative to the design kernel, because correlated shocks create hidden common moments across instruments.

Prior work BorusyakHullJaravel2025ECTJ extends heterogeneous-effect IV interpretations to formula instruments; our seed conjectures that correlated shocks can produce negative formula weights before point identification fails, because the Pi-loaded covariance direction changes the sign of marginal-effect weights while the mean moment remains zero.

Prior work BorusyakHull2023Econometrica characterizes efficient recentered instruments; our seed conjectures that design-IV strictly dominates recentered IV unless epsilon lies in the recentering-null projection, because the efficient design instrument projects out leakage that recentering leaves active.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BorusyakHullJaravel2022REStud/BorusyakHull2023Econometrica/BorusyakHullJaravel2025ECTJ establishes estimator-specific validity conditions; our seed conjectures that the three moments are equivalent iff a spectral Omega-Pi-epsilon threshold is not crossed, because correlated shocks can be absorbed by the design kernel only below a computable singular-value boundary.

anchor_paper.author: Kirill Borusyak, Peter Hull, Xavier Jaravel; Kirill Borusyak, Peter Hull
anchor_paper.claim: These papers give canonical SSIV, recentered-IV, and design-based formula-IV sufficient validity conditions, not an iff frontier among all three.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern, not blocked: the seed asks for a necessary-and-sufficient equality frontier and strict finite-support witnesses, not a routine weakening of assumptions.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from BHJ 2022 and BH 2023 because both already prove formula-IV orthogonality after shock-level recentering.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: those papers do not compare all three limit functionals on the same Omega-Pi-epsilon geometry or define lambda_star.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work AdaoKolesarMorales2019QJE would predict share-induced correlation is primarily an inference problem; our seed conjectures that some correlated-shock directions are harmless for identification while others move the SSIV estimand, because only directions outside the residual exposure kernel survive aggregation through Pi.

anchor_paper.author: Rodrigo Adao, Michal Kolesar, Eduardo Morales
anchor_paper.claim: AKM derive inference valid under arbitrary cross-regional residual correlation in shift-share designs.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/impossibility pattern, not blocked: the target is AKM's named inference workflow, and the obstruction is an open operator class rather than a degenerate witness.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from AKM because their arbitrary-correlation inference already handles correlated shocks.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: AKM address inference under residual correlation, not equality or separation of identifying moments across SSIV variants.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BorusyakHull2023Econometrica assumes recentering removes nonrandom exposure; our seed conjectures that recentering absorbs exactly the leakage directions in a design-null space and fails on the orthogonal Pi-loaded leakage directions, because counterfactual-shock means need not span all covariate-conditional leakage.

anchor_paper.author: Kirill Borusyak, Peter Hull
anchor_paper.claim: BH adjust for nonrandom exposure by subtracting the counterfactual average treatment or instrument.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assumption-boundary pattern, not blocked: the surprise is a projection-null-space classification of leakage, not merely weakening recentering assumptions.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from BH 2023 because recentering is exactly subtracting the conditional mean.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: BH do not classify leakage directions relative to Pi or compare recentered and design-based moments under misspecified leakage.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BorusyakHullJaravel2022REStud treats controls through structured shock-level residualization; our seed conjectures that high-dimensional controls can move an estimator across the equivalence frontier, because partialling out changes Pi and epsilon jointly rather than only precision.

anchor_paper.author: Kirill Borusyak, Peter Hull, Xavier Jaravel
anchor_paper.claim: BHJ derive shock-level controls that isolate conditionally random shock variation under structured controls.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Control-extension pattern, not blocked only if stated as a frontier-crossing theorem; otherwise it would be a routine high-dimensional-controls extension.
seed_details.referee_objection: This follows from standard FWL because controls just residualize the instrument and outcome.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the novelty would be the joint transformation of Omega Pi and epsilon and the resulting equality/separation boundary.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BorusyakHullJaravel2025ECTJ describes optimal formula-IV construction; our seed conjectures a three-way efficiency frontier among canonical, recentered, and design IV under correlated shocks, because covariance weighting changes both the tangent space and the first-stage exposure strength.

anchor_paper.author: Kirill Borusyak, Peter Hull, Xavier Jaravel
anchor_paper.claim: The review describes design-based formula instruments and optimal construction while noting difficult covariance weighting in non-iid settings.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Efficiency-frontier pattern, not blocked: the extension changes the tangent-space object and can reverse dominance rather than merely applying an optimal-instrument recipe.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from semiparametric optimal IV theory because optimal instruments are projections.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: generic optimal IV theory does not specialize to the three named formula-IV estimators under Omega-Pi-epsilon dependence.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work GoldsmithPinkhamSorkinSwift2020AER would interpret Bartik validity through exposure-share exogeneity and Rotemberg weights; our seed conjectures that the share-exogeneity and shock-exogeneity routes diverge exactly when the leading Omega-Pi eigenvector has nonzero Rotemberg leverage, because the same shares can be valid weights but invalid shock aggregators.

anchor_paper.author: Paul Goldsmith-Pinkham, Isaac Sorkin, Henry Swift
anchor_paper.claim: GPSS show typical Bartik use relies on exposure-share exogeneity and Rotemberg weights.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism-decomposition pattern, not blocked: it pits two named identification routes against each other through a sign/loading diagnostic.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from GPSS because Rotemberg weights already describe which shares drive the estimate.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: GPSS do not provide a shock-correlation spectral frontier comparing Bartik with recentered/design formula IV.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work JaegerRuistStuhler2018NBER warns that stable immigrant shares conflate short- and long-run responses; our seed conjectures that this dynamic conflation is the leading-eigenvector witness of the shift-share non-equivalence frontier, because serially persistent origin shocks load on the same Pi direction across periods.

anchor_paper.author: David A. Jaeger, Joakim Ruist, Jan Stuhler
anchor_paper.claim: JRS argue conventional immigration shift-share instruments can conflate short- and long-run responses when settlement patterns are stable.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Phenomenon-form applied mechanism, not blocked: the seed formalizes a named published warning as an eigenvector witness, not a generic dynamic extension.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from JRS because they already show serial stability invalidates the instrument.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: JRS do not derive the Omega-Pi spectral threshold or compare recentered/design formula-IV functionals.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BorusyakHullJaravel2025ECTJ notes multiple formula instruments and overidentification; our seed conjectures that overidentifying restrictions collapse or become informative according to the rank of Pi^{1/2} Omega Pi^{1/2} relative to the design kernel, because correlated shocks create hidden common moments across instruments.

anchor_paper.author: Kirill Borusyak, Peter Hull, Xavier Jaravel
anchor_paper.claim: The review notes multiple treatments/instruments in formula-IV settings and discusses rank conditions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Rank-boundary pattern, not blocked: the proposed object is a rank phase boundary for overidentification, not merely multiple instruments.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from standard overidentification rank conditions.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: standard rank conditions do not include common-shock covariance geometry or recentering/design equality boundaries.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BorusyakHullJaravel2025ECTJ extends heterogeneous-effect IV interpretations to formula instruments; our seed conjectures that correlated shocks can produce negative formula weights before point identification fails, because the Pi-loaded covariance direction changes the sign of marginal-effect weights while the mean moment remains zero.

anchor_paper.author: Kirill Borusyak, Peter Hull, Xavier Jaravel
anchor_paper.claim: The review discusses heterogeneous-effect interpretations and formula-IV weights under monotonicity-style conditions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Heterogeneity/weight-sign pattern, not blocked if it reveals a pre-failure sign frontier; otherwise a routine heterogeneous-effect restatement would be blocked.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from heterogeneous-IV weighting theory.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the proposed sign frontier is tied to correlated formula shocks and Pi, not generic LATE weighting.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BorusyakHull2023Econometrica characterizes efficient recentered instruments; our seed conjectures that design-IV strictly dominates recentered IV unless epsilon lies in the recentering-null projection, because the efficient design instrument projects out leakage that recentering leaves active.

anchor_paper.author: Kirill Borusyak, Peter Hull
anchor_paper.claim: BH discuss efficient construction of recentered instruments under known counterfactual shock designs.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Efficiency/separation pattern, not blocked: the surprise is strict dominance failure/equality boundary, not a routine efficient IV construction.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from BH efficient recentered instruments.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: BH do not state a recentered-versus-design strict dominance theorem under Omega-Pi-epsilon leakage.

proposed_from.literature_map: Stage -1.1 supplied the map: Borusyak-Hull-Jaravel 2022 ReStud is the canonical shock-level SSIV anchor, reducing SSIV orthogonality to shock-level orthogonality under sufficient many-shock conditions (https://doi.org/10.1093/restud/rdab030). Adao-Kolesar-Morales 2019 QJE show exposure-share correlation creates serious inference distortion, but do not classify identification frontiers (https://doi.org/10.1093/qje/qjz025). Goldsmith-Pinkham-Sorkin-Swift 2020 AER give the competing share-exogeneity/Rotemberg-weight interpretation of Bartik instruments (https://doi.org/10.1257/aer.20181047). Borusyak-Hull 2023 Econometrica introduce recentered instruments for nonrandom exposure to exogenous shocks (https://doi.org/10.3982/ECTA19367). Borusyak-Hull-Jaravel 2025 Econometrics Journal review design-based formula IV and note open extensions in non-iid/inference/control settings (https://doi.org/10.1093/ectj/utae003). Jaeger-Ruist-Stuhler 2018 NBER give an applied dynamic shift-share warning in immigration designs (https://doi.org/10.3386/w24285). The active tension is estimator-specific sufficient validity versus a single necessary-and-sufficient geometry over Omega, Pi, and epsilon.

Borusyak-Hull-Jaravel 2025 JEP presents applied shift-share checklists. The active unresolved tension is estimator-by-estimator validity versus a single necessary-and-sufficient geometry for equality or strict separation across canonical, recentered, and design-conditioned moments. banked_reason: Stage -0.5 NO-PASS — all 3 angles REJECT@not-publishable on v1 (immediate, no revise cycle). Recurring C-wellposed defects across pivots: Omega definition normalization wrong (Omega=0 ≠ uncorrelated shocks; uncorrelated normalized shocks yield diagonal not zero), scalar/vector projection mismatch (beta_R-beta_D claimed scalar but defined via vector projection), missing residual shock-loading term needed for beta-equality determination. Proposer didn't adjust across angle pivots. Reviewer also flagged missing Borusyak-Hull 2025 NBER w33594 as a closer comparator. Failure is proposer-side proposal-quality, not rubric incompatibility. Math kernel could be revised, but pivot budget exhausted.
